notes: Headquartered Orem UT. NetWare NOS market leader in 1997. Acquired by Attachmate 2011 for $2.2B; subsequently passed to Micro Focus then OpenText.
Primary competitive threat. Active Directory shipped with Windows 2000 (February 2000). AD ultimately displaced NDS as enterprise directory standard.
Maker of StreetTalk directory service and VINES NOS. Competed with NDS. Sold enterprise division; declined with NetWare market.
Maker of DCE Directory and Security Server; competitor to NDS. Also partnered with Novell on ManageWise/Unicenter integration.
Partnered with Novell to integrate ManageWise with Unicenter TNG. CA's object-oriented database planned for NDS integration.
Agreed to use NDS authentication and management on shared platforms. Aberdeen predicted rolling NDS embedding announcements.
Porting NDS to all versions of Solaris as of 1997. Sun later released its own Sun Directory Services based on LDAP.
Porting NDS to Linux operating system in 1997. Caldera later acquired SCO's Unix business.
Agreed to ship free copy of NDS with every HP/UX platform.
One of three ISPs hosting NDS on the Internet in 1997. Contributing to 500000 new NDS users.
One of three ISPs hosting NDS on the Internet in 1997.
One of three ISPs hosting NDS on the Internet in 1997. Contributing to 500000 new NDS users collectively with AT&T and Deutsche Telekom.
